steps.title: Identify the underlined phrase and its literary device
steps.content: The underlined phrase is "sprouted from the ground like ancient trees in a petrified forest." This is a simile — a direct comparison using like — that describes the physical appearance of the columns at Persepolis. The question asks what function this simile serves in context.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It suggests that the narrator hopes to find a forest nearby.': The narrator never expresses any wish about a forest; this misreads the figurative language as literal desire. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It presents an image to illustrate what one part of the ruins looks like.': The simile compares the columns to trees in a petrified forest, giving the reader a concrete visual of their height and slenderness — exactly illustrating their appearance. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It indicates the specific time in the past when the buildings became ruins.': The simile contains no temporal information; it describes how columns look, not when they fell into ruin. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It emphasizes that the narrator has studied the history of Persepolis.': The passage shows the narrator learning from Sohrab, not displaying prior scholarly knowledge. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
